course_title: COMPUTER CONTROL OF MACHINES AND PROCESSES
description: Discrete control theory reduced to engineering practice through a comprehensive study of discrete system modeling, system identification and digital controller design. Selected industrial processes and machines utilized as subjects on which computer control is to be implemented. Focus: computer control economics and planning as well as the control theory and programming.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: M E 340, 346, or446, graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students
